title: Weather news: Heavy rain, thunderstorm alert in over 15 states; heatwave warning issued for…; Check IMD forecast for Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Bihar, Assam, Punjab, West Bengal, Uttarakhand - news24online.com
full_text:
Weather news: Heavy rain, thunderstorm alert in over 15 states; heatwave warning issued for…; Check IMD forecast for Delhi, Noida, Gurugram, Bihar, Assam, Punjab, West Bengal, Uttarakhand
The southwest monsoon is currently active over large parts of India, with widespread rainfall activity expected across the Himalayan region, eastern India and the Northeast. The IMD also predicts subdued rainfall to lash the plains of northwest, westcentral, and southern Peninsular India during the next 6 to 7 days.
IMD Weather Update: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast active monsoon activity over large parts of North, East, and Northeast India on Sunday, July 12. Heavy rain, accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds, is expected across several states, including Assam, Bihar, Meghalaya, Jharkhand, West Bengal, and Uttarakhand.
Meanwhile, Haryana, Chandigarh, and Delhi-NCR are also likely to receive severe downpours in isolated areas. According to IMD, extremely heavy rainfall may batter northeastern states of Assam, Meghalaya, and Eastern states of Bihar, the Sub-Himalayan West Bengal, and Sikkim.
Heavy rain warning for J&K, Punjab, Odisha, and other states
The Met Department has also forecast the possibility of heavy rainfall over Arunachal Pradesh, Jammu and Kashmir, Chhattisgarh, East Uttar Pradesh, Jharkhand, Punjab, Odisha, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand today.
The southwest monsoon is currently active over large parts of India, with widespread rainfall activity expected across the Himalayan region, eastern India and the Northeast. The IMD also predicts subdued rainfall to lash the plains of northwest, westcentral, and southern Peninsular India during the next 6 to 7 days.
Isolated to scattered rainfall is expected to soak central India, including Madhya Pradesh and Vidarbha, Gujarat, and Maharashtra on Sunday, July 12.
According to IMD's latest outlook, the monsoon will remain active over multiple regions throughout next week, indicating that rain-bearing systems will continue to affect eastern, northeastern and parts of western India. States, including Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal, Konkan and Goa, coastal Karnataka, and Uttarakhand, will continue receiving rainfall over the next several days.
Thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds with speeds up to 40-50 kmph are expected over J&K, the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, East Madhya Pradesh, and Jharkhand.
Gusty winds of 30-40 kmph are also likely to hit Gangetic West Bengal, Odisha and West Madhya Pradesh, while thunderstorms are expected across parts of the Northeast, Bihar and Uttarakhand.
Heatwave warning
While most of India will remain under monsoon conditions, heat wave conditions are very likely in isolated pockets over Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam on 12th July.
